spanish: Aunque algunos son bienvenidos, la mayoría no.
english: Although some are welcome, most are not.
explanation: Subjunctive mood not needed here, but uses 'aunque' (although) with indicative for factual contrast between two groups

spanish: Si la cosa funciona, dos células se unirán después de un complicado baile.
english: If things work out, two cells will join after a complicated dance.
explanation: First conditional - 'si' + present tense, future tense in main clause for real/likely situations

spanish: Es uno de los motivos de que todos los años tengamos que combatir enfermedades como la gripe.
english: It's one of the reasons why every year we have to fight diseases like the flu.
explanation: Present subjunctive after 'motivos de que' - expressing necessity/obligation with emotional reaction trigger

spanish: Si el proceso se equivoca y escapan células que reconocen nuestras propias piezas proteicas, pueden originarse enfermedades autoinmunes.
english: If the process fails and cells that recognize our own protein pieces escape, autoimmune diseases can develop.
explanation: Complex relative clause with 'que' + subjunctive implication in conditional context, plus passive-like construction 'pueden originarse'
